key_insight: An agentic multi-LLM pipeline can autoformalize research-level mathematics in Lean 4 by extending missing concepts and verifying them with auxiliary lemmas before proving the main theorem.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the bottleneck of turning paper mathematics into machine-checked code, enabling more reliable theorem proving workflows for advanced research and library-gap heavy domains.
method: Orchestrator coordinates a multi-agent formalization pipeline.
Dynamically extends missing type definitions beyond Mathlib.
Uses an Auxiliary Lemma validation step before main theorem formalization.
evidence_signal: The system was evaluated on 32 PutnamBench problems and five STOC papers, with machine-checked Lean proofs for all five papers and two proofs requiring no axioms beyond Lean's kernel.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the agent orchestration and on bridging gaps in Lean libraries, which can be brittle for unfamiliar or highly specialized mathematics.
paper_title: Beyond the Library: An Agentic Framework for Autoformalizing Research Mathematics
